title: Who we are and how to contact us - Canada.ca
content:
## Who we are and how to contact us
Where a person considers that the actions of Canada or a treaty partner result or may result in taxation not in accordance with a particular treaty, the person may request competent authority assistance. Assistance by the competent authority is generally provided under the mutual agreement procedure (MAP) article contained in Canada's tax treaties .
Canada's income tax treaties define the Canadian competent authority as the Minister of National Revenue or the Minister's authorized representatives.
The competent authority
- resolves double taxation cases and cases of taxation not in accordance with a treaty; and
- negotiates advanced pricing arrangements (APA).
The competent authority also
- resolves issues concerning the application of tax treaties generally where specific taxpayers are not involved and interpretive issues involving treaty partners;
- considers the recognition of foreign pension plans allowed under certain treaties; and
- considers applications under paragraph 6, of Article XXIX A (Limitation on Benefits) in the Canada-US Tax Convention.
For more details, refer to IC71-17, Guidance on Competent Authority Assistance Under Canada's Tax Conventions .

## Submit an Enquiry or/and Request for Assistance
This web form will allow you to submit an enquiry and/or request for assistance for MAP or APA requests.
The purpose of the web form is to assist taxpayers in establishing a secure method of communication to submit an enquiry or a request for the following services:
- Mutual Agreement Procedure (MAP) – Transfer Pricing
- Mutual Agreement Procedure (MAP) – Non-transfer Pricing
- Mutual Agreement Procedure (MAP) – Capital Gains Deferral Agreement
- Mutual Agreement Procedure (MAP) – Spousal Trust Agreement
- Mutual Agreement Procedure (MAP) – S-Corporation Agreement
- Mutual Agreement Procedure (MAP) – Other
- Advance Pricing Agreements (APA)
The web form will ensure that all requests are directed to a monitored electronic mailbox. An official of the CASD will then establish a secure method of electronic communication to allow taxpayers and their representatives to submit additional information pertaining to their request. This initiative will ensure taxpayer information is protected while facilitating timely access to services offered by the CASD.
Note: This form does not allow you to submit additional supporting documentation.
CASD will contact you for additional documentation required to complete your application. A MAP or APA request will not be considered complete until all required documentation is received.
Once you complete the form, we recommend that you print or save a copy of the confirmation for your records.
For more information regarding double taxation cases, advance pricing arrangements, and elections with respect to U.S. Roth Individual Retirement Accounts (Roth IRAs), contact us at:
International and Large Business Directorate Competent Authority Services Division 344 Slater Street 18th Floor, Canada Building Ottawa ON K1A 0L5
email: CPMAPAPAG@cra-arc.gc.ca (do not send confidential information)
